details.text: Derivative: يَذْرَؤُ from ذَرَأَ which means to make or create something and spread it. It holds the meaning to spread, disseminate and multiply.
Derivative: مَقَالِيدُ – Refers to the keys to the vaults of treasures.
Derivative: تَتَفَرَّقُوا from تَفَرَّقَ means to fall into division amongst one another i.e., to become divided.
Derivative: أَقِيمُوا۟ from أَقَامَ – يُقِيمُ – إِقَامَة. It means to make something (else) stand and to establish its presence.

Derivative: شَرَعَ – In classical Arabic they would say شَرَعَ الإِبِل which meant to take the camel to water (i.e., to drink from it). From this شَريعَة was used to refer to a source of water to drink from. As such the شَريعَة in terms of it being the laws laid down for His servants to follow is viewed to represent the source of life. شَرَعَ means to make clear and legislate or prescribe and set down as law.

The significance of the use of مَا and الَّذِي in this verse and the difference between the two – The significance of the use of مَا (what or whatever) in this verse is that مَا refers to something that is general and not defined, in contrast to الَّذِي (what) which implies exactness. The use of مَا in respect to what was revealed to Nuh (as) and thereafter the other Prophets (as) therefore signifies that we don’t know precisely what was given to Nuh (as) and the other Prophets, whilst the use of ٱلَّذِىٓ in relation to what was revealed to the Prophet ﷺ signifies that we know precisely what was revealed to him.
- كَبُرَ عَلَى – To be a big or major matter i.e., difficult and hard to swallow or accept.

Derivative: بَغْيًۢا from بَغَى at refers to acting unjustly to others and wronging them to gain the upper hand over them. In this verse the linkage to knowledge suggests that knowledge was used as a weapon to gain the upper hand over others.
Derivative: أَعْدِلَ from عَدَلَ – عَدْلٌ. عَدَلَ means to act or deal fairly and justly. عَدْلٌ is public, open and undisputed justice.
Derivative: دَاحِضَةٌ from دَحَضَ which means to be invalidated, refuted or rendered useless or worthless.
Derivative: يُمَارُونَ from مَارَى – يُمارِي – مُماراة and it means to argue based upon a doubt rather than conviction or firm evidence.
Derivative: لَطِيفٌۢ – Means carting and subtle or delicate.
it is unique in the qur’an for the second verse to also be from the huroof al-muqatta’aat (الحُرُوفُ المُقَطَّعَات)

Derivative: يَقْتَرِفْ from اِقْتَرَفَ and it means to earn something evil, bad or disgusting. To perpetrate or commit a criminal act. Here, it is used simply to mean to earn or to commit a Hasanah (good deed).
Derivative: يُحِقُّ from أَحَقَّ means to establish and prove the truthfulness of something.
Derivative: يُوبِقْ from أَوْبَقَ means to make perish and bring about ruin. In this verse it refers to causing them to drown.

Derivative: الْفَوَاحِشَ is the plural of فَاحِشَة and refers to the various forms or grades of shameless immoral conduct, the most prominent of which is Zina (fornication and adultery). It is mentioned specifically in this verse after the mention of كَبَائِرَ الْإِثْمِ (the major acts of sinfulness) even though it is included within them in order to draw extra attention to it.

Derivative: يَجْتَنِبُونَ from اِجْتَنَبَ which means to stay away from and to avoid something. It comes from جَنْب which means “side” and means to step to the side of something or to put something to the side i.e., to avoid it.

Derivative: رَزَقْنَا from رَزَقَ – رِزْقٌ and it means to provide sustenance and provisions. رِزْقٌ encompasses more than money and wealth. It can also include things such as health, time and youthfulness. To spend (إِنْفَاق) from these provisions means to give from them.
Derivative: يَنتَصِرُونَ from اِنْتَصَرَ – اِنْتِصار which means to retaliate and seek vengeance to gain the upper hand or justice after being wronged.

- Literally “No doubt that is from the matters (الْأُمُور) of great resolve (عَزْمِ)”. It refers to the deeds of the highest calibre and quality i.e., those Deeds which require a great deal of resolve and as a consequence they earn a great reward.
- إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمِنْ عَزْمِ الْأُمُور when mentioned in the Qur’an it is always related to صَبْر (patience). The only difference is that in this verse an extra لام (لَ) is added to the مِن (Min - from) to add emphasis to it as if the matter is even greater in this context. A possible explanation is that here a choice is being provided where both are accepted in terms of retaliating or remaining patient and forgiving, whilst the patience in the other verses is necessary without choice.
Derivative: مُّقِيمٍ refers to a permanent place to reside in.

Derivative: نَّكِيرٍ refers to denial or a denier. It can be understood to be attributed to a third person i.e., someone who denies on your behalf and stands up for you, or according to the majority view, it refers to the people themselves not being able to deny what they have done in terms of disbelief and sinful acts on the Day of Judgement.
Derivative: مَّلْجَإٍ from لَجَأَ which means to resort to or find refuge in.

Derivative: رُوحًا can refer to many things including the رُوح (soul/spirit) inside us and in this verse, it has been said to refer to the revelation (وَحْي - Wahy), Jibreel (as) and the Qur’an amongst other meanings.

- تَهْدِي إِلَىٰ – “You guide to”. The use of إِلَى with هَدَى (to guide) means to guide or direct towards something. هَدَى by itself (to guide) means the accomplishment of the result whilst هَدَى إلَى means to guide or direct towards that without any guarantee of accomplishment. The Prophet ﷺ guides us to a straight path but cannot accomplish the guidance upon us.

Derivative: الْأُمُورُ – (All) matters. The singular of أُمُور which is أَمْر (Amr) can refer to a matter, affair and can also refer to a command and decision i.e., all the decisions we make in this life will also return to Allah to pass judgment upon.

- أُمَّ الْقُرَىٰ literally means the “Mother of cities or towns” and it refers to Makkah which was the effective social, linguistic, economic and political capital of the region. It can also refer to Makkah being the first city in the case where Adam (as) first laid the foundations of the Ka’bah, just as it could imply Makkah being recognised as the final capital of the world before the Day of Judgement.
